docs.title: Home
docs.text:
An easy-to-use ntfy python library.

Note

The documentation is a work in progress. If you see something missing or incorrect, please feel free to open a pull request.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: To get going with some basic functionality, see the quickstart guide.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: For examples of common use-cases, check out the examples page.

docs.title: Reference
docs.text: For a full API reference, see the reference page.

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Github
  • PyPi

docs.title: Send a basic message
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

client.send("Your message here")

docs.title: Send a markdown formatted message
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

message = """# My Message

- Bullet
- Points
"""

client.send(message, format_as_markdown=True)

docs.title: Send a message with an attachment
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

client.send("Your message here", attachment="/path/to/your/file.txt")

docs.title: Send a message with priority
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

client.send("Your message here", priority=client.MessagePriority.HIGH)

docs.title: Send a message with tags
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

client.send("Your message here", tags=["tag1", "tag2"])

docs.title: Send a message with click action
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

client.send("Your message here", actions=[client.ViewAction(label="Open Website", url="https://ntfy.sh"),])

docs.title: Quickstart Guide
docs.text:
1. Install using pip with pip3 install python-ntfy
  2. Configure the following environment variables:
    • NTFY_USER: The username for your server (if required)
    • NTFY_PASSWORD: The password for your server (if required)
    • NTFY_SERVER: The server URL (defaults to https://ntft.sh)
  3. Setup your application to use the library:
# Import the ntfy client
from python_ntfy import NtfyClient

# Create an `NtfyClient` instance with a topic
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

# Send a message
client.send("Your message here")

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: NtfyClient is a Python package for interacting with the ntfy.sh service or a self-hosted ntfy server. It provides functionality to send notifications and retrieve cached messages.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
You can install NtfyClient using pip:

pip install ntfyclient

docs.title: Initializing the Client
docs.text:
from ntfyclient import NtfyClient

client = NtfyClient(topic="your_topic", server="https://ntfy.sh")

docs.title: __init__(topic: str, server: str = "https://ntfy.sh")
docs.text:
Initialize a new NtfyClient instance.

  • topic: The topic to use for this client
  • server: The server to connect to. Must include the protocol (http/https)

docs.title: set_topic(topic: str)
docs.text:
Set a new topic for the client.

  • topic: The new topic to use for this client

docs.title: send(message: str, title: str = None, priority: Optional[MessagePriority] = MessagePriority.DEFAULT, tags: list = [], actions: list[Union[ViewAction, BroadcastAction, HttpAction, None]] = [], format_as_markdown: bool = False)
docs.text:
Send a text-based message to the server.

  • message: The message to send
  • title: The title of the message (optional)
  • priority: The priority of the message (optional, defaults to MessagePriority.DEFAULT)
  • tags: A list of tags to attach to the message (optional)
  • actions: A list of Action objects to attach to the message (optional)
  • format_as_markdown: If true, the message will be formatted as markdown (optional)

Returns the response from the server.

docs.title: send_file(file: str, title: str = None, priority: Optional[MessagePriority] = MessagePriority.DEFAULT, tags: list = [], actions: list[Union[ViewAction, BroadcastAction, HttpAction, None]] = [])
docs.text:
Send a file to the server.

  • file: The path to the file to send
  • title: The title of the file (optional)
  • priority: The priority of the message (optional, defaults to MessagePriority.DEFAULT)
  • tags: A list of tags to attach to the message (optional)
  • actions: A list of Action objects to attach to the message (optional)

Returns the response from the server.

docs.title: get_cached_messages(since: str = "all", scheduled: bool = False)
docs.text:
Get cached messages from the server.

  • since: The timestamp to start from. If set to "all", will return all messages (optional)
  • scheduled: If true, will return scheduled messages (optional)

Returns a list of messages.

docs.title: MessagePriority
docs.text:
An enumeration of message priority levels:

  • MIN: "1"
  • LOW: "2"
  • DEFAULT: "3"
  • HIGH: "4"
  • MAX: "5"
  • URGENT: Alias for MAX

docs.title: ActionType
docs.text:
An enumeration of action button types:

  • VIEW: "view"
  • BROADCAST: "broadcast"
  • HTTP: "http"

docs.title: ViewAction
docs.text:
Represents a view action button.

  • __init__(label: str, url: str, clear: bool = False)

docs.title: BroadcastAction
docs.text:
Represents a broadcast action button.

  • __init__(label: str, intent: str = "io.heckel.ntfy.USER_ACTION", extras: Optional[dict] = None, clear: bool = False)

docs.title: HttpAction
docs.text:
Represents an HTTP action button.

  • __init__(label: str, url: str, method: str = "POST", headers: Optional[dict] = None, body: Optional[str] = None, clear: bool = False)

docs.title: Sending a Simple Message
docs.text:
from ntfyclient import NtfyClient, MessagePriority

client = NtfyClient("your_topic")
response = client.send(
    message="Hello, world!",
    title="Test Message",
    priority=MessagePriority.HIGH,
    tags=["test", "example"]
)
print(response)

docs.title: Sending a File
docs.text:
from ntfyclient import NtfyClient

client = NtfyClient("your_topic")
response = client.send_file("path/to/your/file.txt", title="Important File")
print(response)

docs.title: Retrieving Cached Messages
docs.text:
from ntfyclient import NtfyClient

client = NtfyClient("your_topic")
messages = client.get_cached_messages(since="2023-01-01")
for message in messages:
    print(f"Message: {message['message']}, Time: {message['time']}")

docs.title: Using Action Buttons
docs.text:
from ntfyclient import NtfyClient, ViewAction, BroadcastAction, HttpAction

client = NtfyClient("your_topic")
actions = [
    ViewAction("Open Website", "https://example.com"),
    BroadcastAction("Custom Action", extras={"key": "value"}),
    HttpAction("API Call", "https://api.example.com", method="POST", body='{"key": "value"}')
]
response = client.send(
    message="Message with actions",
    title="Action Test",
    actions=actions
)
print(response)

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
The NtfyClient supports the following environment variables:

  • NTFY_SERVER: Sets the default server URL
  • NTFY_USER: Sets the username for authentication
  • NTFY_PASSWORD: Sets the password for authentication

If both NTFY_USER and NTFY_PASSWORD are set, the client will use them for authentication.

docs.title: Initialiser
docs.text:
from python_ntfy import NtfyClient

NtfyClient(
  topic: str,
  server: str = "https://ntfy.sh"
)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
topic (required)

Type: String The topic to send messages to.

server

Type: String Default: https://ntfy.sh The server to send messages to.

docs.title: send
docs.text:
NtfyClient.send(
  message: str,
  title: str = None,
  priority: Optional[MessagePriority] = MessagePriority.DEFAULT,
  tags: list = [],
  actions: list[Union[ViewAction, BroadcastAction, HttpAction, None]] = [],
  format_as_markdown: bool = False,
) -> dict

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
message (required)

Type: String The message to send to the topic.

title

Type: String Default: None The title of the message. If None, the message will have no title.

priority

Type: Optional[MessagePriority] Default: MessagePriority.DEFAULT The priority of the message. Can be one of the values from the MessagePriority enum.

tags

Type: list Default: [] A list of tags to associate with the message.

actions

Type: list[Union[ViewAction, BroadcastAction, HttpAction, None]] Default: [] A list of actions to include with the message. Each action can be an instance of ViewAction, BroadcastAction, HttpAction, or None.

format_as_markdown

Type: bool Default: False If True, the message will be formatted as Markdown.

docs.title: send_file
docs.text:
NtfyClient.send_file(
    file: str,
    title: str = None,
    priority: Optional[MessagePriority] = MessagePriority.DEFAULT,
    tags: list = [],
    actions: list[Union[ViewAction, BroadcastAction, HttpAction, None]] = [],
) -> dict

docs.title: set_topic
docs.text: NtfyClient.set_topic(topic: str) -> None

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: topic (required) Type: str The topic to send messages to.

docs.title: get_cached_messages
docs.text:
NtfyClient.get_cached_messages(
    since: str = "all",
    scheduled: bool = False
)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
since

Type: str Default: "all" The timestamp to start retrieving messages from. If set to "all", will return all messages.

scheduled

Type: bool Default: False If True, will return scheduled messages.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Type: list A list of dictionaries containing the cached messages.

docs.title: ViewAction
docs.text:
ViewAction(
    label: str,
    url: str,
    clear: bool = False
)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
label (required)

Type: str The label for the view action button.

url (required)

Type: str The URL to open when the action button is clicked.

clear

Type: bool Default: False If True, the notification will be cleared when the action is tapped.

docs.title: BroadcastAction
docs.text:
BroadcastAction(
    label: str,
    intent: str = "io.heckel.ntfy.USER_ACTION",
    extras: Optional[dict] = None,
    clear: bool = False
)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
label (required)

Type: str The label for the broadcast action button.

intent

Type: str Default: "io.heckel.ntfy.USER_ACTION" The Android intent to broadcast when the action is tapped.

extras

Type: Optional[dict] Default: None Additional key-value pairs to add to the broadcast intent.

clear

Type: bool Default: False If True, the notification will be cleared when the action is tapped.

docs.title: HttpAction
docs.text:
HttpAction(
    label: str,
    url: str,
    method: str = "POST",
    headers: Optional[dict] = None,
    body: Optional[str] = None,
    clear: bool = False
)

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
label (required)

Type: str The label for the HTTP action button.

url (required)

Type: str The URL to send the HTTP request to when the action is tapped.

method

Type: str Default: "POST" The HTTP method to use for the request.

headers

Type: Optional[dict] Default: None Additional headers to include in the HTTP request.

body

Type: Optional[str] Default: None The body of the HTTP request.

clear

Type: bool Default: False If True, the notification will be cleared when the action is tapped.

docs.title: MessagePriority
docs.text:
An enumeration of message priority levels.

class MessagePriority(Enum):
    MIN = "1"
    LOW = "2"
    DEFAULT = "3"
    HIGH = "4"
    MAX = "5"
    URGENT = MAX

docs.title: ActionType
docs.text:
An enumeration of action button types.

class ActionType(Enum):
    VIEW = "view"
    BROADCAST = "broadcast"
    HTTP = "http"
